project.creator: Sơn
environment: 1. Frontend: React wizard and request flows
project.notes: Strict feature-based workbook for UC-12 Request Marketplace Publishing. Result values are based on the local Jest execution performed on 23/03/2026 against the project-requests marketplace flow spec.

changeLog.description: Initial feature/function-based workbook generated from repository implementation, sequence flow, and executed Jest evidence.
changeLog.reference: Wizard flow, project-requests module README, and project-requests.service.spec.ts

features.name: Request Marketplace Publishing
features.testRequirement: Validate wizard-based request creation, visibility switching, marketplace publication, broker application, broker acceptance, and draft deletion safeguards for the UC-12 marketplace publishing flow.

functions.name: Create Request for Marketplace
functions.description: Client submits wizard data and the service persists a request in public or saved-draft mode.
functions.preCondition: Client is authenticated, required wizard fields are available, and create-request quota allows submission.

functions.name: Toggle Marketplace Visibility
functions.description: Client switches an existing request between public and private draft visibility before broker assignment.
functions.preCondition: Client owns the request, the request is still a draft, and no broker is assigned.

functions.name: Publish Request to Marketplace
functions.description: Client publishes an existing draft request to the marketplace by normalizing status to PUBLIC_DRAFT.
functions.preCondition: Client owns the request, request status is DRAFT, PRIVATE_DRAFT, or PUBLIC_DRAFT, and no broker is assigned.

functions.name: Broker Application Submission
functions.description: Broker applies to a public marketplace request and the system enforces uniqueness and visibility rules.
functions.preCondition: Request is PUBLIC_DRAFT with no assigned broker unless the negative case intentionally violates those rules.

functions.name: Accept Broker Application
functions.description: Client accepts one broker application and closes the remaining pending proposals.
functions.preCondition: Client owns the request and at least one broker proposal exists unless the negative case uses an invalid request state.

functions.name: Delete Draft Request Safely
functions.description: Client deletes a draft request only when ownership and marketplace safety rules still permit removal.
functions.preCondition: Request exists in a draft status and the client is the owner unless the negative case intentionally violates those rules.

testCases.description: Create a new marketplace request successfully from the wizard in public mode.
testCases.procedure: 1. Open the request wizard as an authenticated client.\n2. Enter valid title, description, budget, timeline, and wizard answers.\n3. Submit with marketplace/public mode so the payload is not treated as a saved draft.
testCases.expectedResults:
1. The service checks create-request quota.
2. A request is saved with status PUBLIC_DRAFT.
3. Answers are persisted.
4. Audit create and quota increment are recorded.
testCases.actualResults: Executed Jest case passed: the service created the request in PUBLIC_DRAFT, persisted two wizard answers, called auditLogsService.logCreate, incremented CREATE_REQUEST quota usage, and returned the hydrated request.
testCases.dependencies: Authenticated client account, available create-request quota, and a valid wizard payload.
testCases.note: Evidence: create -> creates a new marketplace request in PUBLIC_DRAFT when isDraft is false.

testCases.description: Create and save a request as draft when the client does not publish immediately.
testCases.procedure: 1. Open the request wizard as an authenticated client.\n2. Provide the minimum draft payload.\n3. Submit in saved-draft mode with isDraft = true.
testCases.expectedResults: 1. The request is created with status DRAFT.\n2. The service returns the saved draft.\n3. No answer rows are created when no wizard answers are supplied.
testCases.actualResults: Executed Jest case passed: the service created the request in DRAFT, skipped answer creation, and returned the saved draft request.
testCases.dependencies: Authenticated client account, available create-request quota, and a valid draft payload.
testCases.note: Evidence: create -> creates a draft request in DRAFT when isDraft is true.

testCases.description: Switch a public draft request to private draft and reject pending broker proposals.
testCases.procedure: 1. Load a PUBLIC_DRAFT request owned by the client.\n2. Ensure the request has pending broker proposals.\n3. Update the request status to PRIVATE_DRAFT.
testCases.expectedResults: 1. Pending broker proposals are loaded.\n2. Each pending proposal is marked REJECTED.\n3. The request is saved as PRIVATE_DRAFT.
testCases.actualResults: Executed Jest case passed: pending broker proposals were queried, both pending proposals were saved as rejected, and the request status changed to PRIVATE_DRAFT.
testCases.dependencies: Existing PUBLIC_DRAFT request with pending broker proposals and client ownership.
testCases.note: Evidence: update -> switches PUBLIC_DRAFT to PRIVATE_DRAFT and rejects pending broker proposals.

testCases.description: Switch a private draft request back to public draft without rejecting proposals.
testCases.procedure: 1. Load a PRIVATE_DRAFT request owned by the client.\n2. Update the request status to PUBLIC_DRAFT.
testCases.expectedResults: 1. The request is saved as PUBLIC_DRAFT.\n2. No pending-proposal rejection flow is triggered.
testCases.actualResults: Executed Jest case passed: the request was saved as PUBLIC_DRAFT and the broker proposal rejection lookup was not called.
testCases.dependencies: Existing PRIVATE_DRAFT request and client ownership.
testCases.note: Evidence: update -> switches PRIVATE_DRAFT to PUBLIC_DRAFT without rejecting proposals.

testCases.description: Publish a client-owned DRAFT request to PUBLIC_DRAFT successfully.
testCases.procedure: 1. Load a DRAFT request owned by the client.\n2. Call the publish flow for that request.\n3. Refresh the request after saving.
testCases.expectedResults: 1. The request status becomes PUBLIC_DRAFT.\n2. Audit logging records the DRAFT to PUBLIC_DRAFT change.\n3. The published request is returned.
testCases.actualResults: Executed Jest case passed: the service saved the request as PUBLIC_DRAFT, logged the DRAFT to PUBLIC_DRAFT audit update, and returned the published request.
testCases.dependencies: Existing DRAFT request owned by the client with no broker assigned.
testCases.note: Evidence: publish -> publishes a client-owned draft request to PUBLIC_DRAFT.

testCases.description: Return the existing request unchanged when it is already PUBLIC_DRAFT.
testCases.procedure: 1. Load a request already in PUBLIC_DRAFT.\n2. Call the publish flow again.
testCases.expectedResults: 1. The service returns the request unchanged.\n2. No additional save operation occurs.\n3. No audit update is written.
testCases.actualResults: Executed Jest case passed: the service returned the existing PUBLIC_DRAFT request, skipped saving, and skipped audit update logging.
testCases.dependencies: Existing PUBLIC_DRAFT request owned by the client with no broker assigned.
testCases.note: Evidence: publish -> returns the request unchanged when it is already PUBLIC_DRAFT.

testCases.description: Reject publishing when the request is in a non-publishable state.
testCases.procedure: 1. Load a request in BROKER_ASSIGNED or another non-publishable state.\n2. Call the publish flow.
testCases.expectedResults: 1. The service throws BadRequestException.\n2. The request is not saved.\n3. The publish operation is aborted.
testCases.actualResults: Executed Jest case passed: publishing from BROKER_ASSIGNED raised BadRequestException and no save operation was performed.
testCases.dependencies: Existing request owned by the client in a non-publishable status such as BROKER_ASSIGNED.
testCases.note: Evidence: publish -> rejects publish from a non-publishable status.

testCases.description: Create a pending broker proposal for a PUBLIC_DRAFT request and track quota usage.
testCases.procedure: 1. Load a PUBLIC_DRAFT request with no assigned broker.\n2. Submit a broker cover letter for the request.\n3. Persist the proposal.
testCases.expectedResults:
1. Application quota is checked.
2. A broker proposal is created with status PENDING.
3. Application usage is incremented.
4. The saved proposal is returned.
testCases.actualResults: Executed Jest case passed: quota check ran, a PENDING broker proposal was created and saved, APPLY_TO_REQUEST quota usage was incremented, and the created proposal was returned.
testCases.dependencies: Existing PUBLIC_DRAFT request, broker authentication, no assigned broker, and an available application slot.
testCases.note: Evidence: applyToRequest -> creates a pending broker proposal for a PUBLIC_DRAFT request and tracks quota usage.

testCases.description: Reject a duplicate broker application for the same request.
testCases.procedure: 1. Load a PUBLIC_DRAFT request.\n2. Ensure the same broker already has a proposal on the request.\n3. Submit another application.
testCases.expectedResults: 1. The service throws BadRequestException.\n2. Quota is not checked.\n3. No new proposal is created.
testCases.actualResults: Executed Jest case passed: the duplicate proposal triggered BadRequestException and the service skipped quota checking and proposal creation.
testCases.dependencies: Existing PUBLIC_DRAFT request with an existing proposal from the same broker.
testCases.note: Evidence: applyToRequest -> rejects duplicate broker applications for the same request.

testCases.description: Reject a broker application when the request is not public.
testCases.procedure: 1. Load a request in PRIVATE_DRAFT.\n2. Submit a broker application.
testCases.expectedResults: 1. The service throws BadRequestException because the request is not open for marketplace applications.\n2. Proposal lookup and creation do not continue.
testCases.actualResults: Executed Jest case passed: the PRIVATE_DRAFT request caused BadRequestException and the broker proposal existence lookup was not called.
testCases.dependencies: Existing non-public request such as PRIVATE_DRAFT.
testCases.note: Evidence: applyToRequest -> rejects applications when the request is not public.

testCases.description: Reject a broker application when the target request does not exist.
testCases.procedure: 1. Call the broker application flow with a missing request id.
testCases.expectedResults: 1. The service throws NotFoundException.\n2. No proposal is created.
testCases.actualResults: Executed Jest case passed: the missing request produced NotFoundException and the application flow stopped.
testCases.dependencies: A request id that does not exist.
testCases.note: Evidence: applyToRequest -> rejects applications when the request does not exist.

testCases.description: Accept a selected broker, assign the broker to the request, and reject the other pending proposals.
testCases.procedure: 1. Load a request with pending broker proposals.\n2. Accept one broker proposal as the owning client.\n3. Persist the assignment and proposal outcomes.
testCases.expectedResults:
1. The selected broker is assigned to the request.
2. Request status changes to BROKER_ASSIGNED.
3. The selected proposal becomes ACCEPTED.
4. Other pending proposals become REJECTED.
5. The final request is returned.
testCases.actualResults: Executed Jest case passed: the request was saved with brokerId and BROKER_ASSIGNED, the selected proposal was updated to ACCEPTED, the other pending proposal was saved as REJECTED, and the final request was returned.
testCases.dependencies: Existing request with pending broker proposals and client ownership.
testCases.note: Evidence: acceptBroker -> assigns the selected broker, marks the request BROKER_ASSIGNED, and rejects the other pending proposals.

testCases.description: Reject broker acceptance when the request is in an invalid state.
testCases.procedure: 1. Load a request in COMPLETED or another invalid state for broker acceptance.\n2. Attempt to accept a broker.
testCases.expectedResults: 1. The service throws an invalid-state error.\n2. The request is not saved.\n3. Broker proposal updates do not run.
testCases.actualResults: Executed Jest case passed: attempting to accept a broker from COMPLETED raised the invalid-state error and no save or proposal update occurred.
testCases.dependencies: Existing request in an invalid state such as COMPLETED.
testCases.note: Evidence: acceptBroker -> rejects broker acceptance from an invalid request state.

testCases.description: Delete a draft-status request owned by the caller successfully.
testCases.procedure: 1. Load a draft-status request owned by the client.\n2. Verify there is no accepted freelancer proposal.\n3. Run the delete flow.
testCases.expectedResults:
1. Related answers are deleted.
2. Related broker and freelancer proposals are deleted.
3. The request is removed.
4. Audit delete is recorded.
5. A success response is returned.
testCases.actualResults: Executed Jest case passed: answer, broker proposal, and freelancer proposal rows were deleted, the request was removed, auditLogsService.logDelete was called, and the service returned success.
testCases.dependencies: Existing draft-status request owned by the client with no accepted freelancer proposal.
testCases.note: Evidence: deleteRequest -> deletes a draft request owned by the caller.

testCases.description: Reject deletion when the caller does not own the request.
testCases.procedure: 1. Load a request owned by another client.\n2. Attempt to delete it as a different client.
testCases.expectedResults: 1. The service throws ForbiddenException.\n2. The request is not deleted.
testCases.actualResults: Executed Jest case passed: deleting another client's request raised ForbiddenException.
testCases.dependencies: Existing request owned by another client.
testCases.note: Evidence: deleteRequest -> rejects deleting another client request.

testCases.description: Reject deletion after a broker has already been assigned.
testCases.procedure: 1. Load a request in BROKER_ASSIGNED with brokerId set.\n2. Attempt to delete it as the owner.
testCases.expectedResults: 1. The service throws BadRequestException.\n2. The request is not deleted.
testCases.actualResults: Executed Jest case passed: deleting a request after broker assignment raised BadRequestException and the delete flow stopped.
testCases.dependencies: Existing request with brokerId set and status such as BROKER_ASSIGNED.
testCases.note: Evidence: deleteRequest -> rejects deletion after a broker has been assigned.

testCases.description: Reject deletion when the request has an accepted freelancer proposal.
testCases.procedure: 1. Load a draft-status request owned by the client.\n2. Ensure an ACCEPTED freelancer proposal exists.\n3. Attempt to delete the request.
testCases.expectedResults: 1. The service throws BadRequestException.\n2. The request is not deleted while an accepted freelancer exists.
testCases.actualResults: Executed Jest case passed: the accepted freelancer proposal blocked deletion with BadRequestException.
testCases.dependencies: Existing draft-status request owned by the client with an ACCEPTED freelancer proposal.
testCases.note: Evidence: deleteRequest -> rejects deletion when an accepted freelancer exists.
